questions.prompt: Your assistant filtered an important email into the low-priority bucket silently. User found it weeks later and lost trust. Fix?

choices.text: Make filtering transparent: every filtered item appears in the activity log with the rule that triggered it; user can override per item or per rule; periodic recap mentions filtered items. Hidden actions are the fastest path to trust crises — surface them as standard, not exceptions.

questions.explanation: Silent actions seem efficient but burn trust on the first mistake. Transparent filtering shows what was filtered, why, and lets the user override. The action stays useful; the trust stays intact.

questions.prompt: What three transparency surfaces should an autonomous assistant offer?

choices.text: In-the-moment narration (what's happening now), persistent activity log with the 'why' for each action, and a periodic recap (weekly summary of work done). Each serves a different purpose; together they make a black-box assistant glass-box.

questions.explanation: Three surfaces: live narration during interactive sessions, persistent log for retrospective review, periodic recap for orientation. Each addresses a different user moment — and together they build durable trust.
